content:
Make sure to use transitional words and phrases (such as "in addition," "however," "on the other hand," etc.) throughout your essay so that it flows smoothly from one point to the next.

Conclude your essay by restating your thesis statement and showing how your comparisons support it.Compare refers to both similarities and differences. Contrast only refers to similarities.(Source: PEDIAALinks to an external site.)
Long Description

After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Organize the Compare and Contrast Essay Point-by-Point.

Week 4 | Organize Compare and Contrast Essay Point-by-Point

Organize Compare and Contrast Essay Point-by-Point
There are two main ways to organize a compare-and-contrast essay. The first is by comparing the subjects point by point.

In this option for compare-and-contrast essay writing, you take each point one at a time. Start with the first point, then move on, and make comparisons for the second. Repeat this process until you've reached the end of your essay. In this structure, it's important to remember that your goal is to emphasize the similarities and differences between the two subjects; don't let one side or the other dominate your paper.
